# ScreenX

> ScreenX is an open-source native macOS window manager for arranging app windows with Command-drag previews, quick layout hotkeys, per-screen grids, and saved workspaces.

## Product

- [Homepage](https://screenx.scrubmac.app/): Product overview, screenshots, hotkeys, install notes, privacy notes, FAQ, and latest download.
- [Download latest DMG](https://screenx.scrubmac.app/download): Current ScreenX installer for macOS.
- [Releases](https://screenx.scrubmac.app/releases): Current and older signed, notarized release downloads with SHA256 checksums.
- [Update feed](https://screenx.scrubmac.app/api/update): JSON update endpoint used by the macOS app.
- [Release API](https://screenx.scrubmac.app/api/releases): JSON release archive for all public builds.
- [Source code](https://github.com/SyntaxFear/screenX): Public open-source macOS app repository.
- [Privacy](https://screenx.scrubmac.app/privacy): Privacy details for the website and native app.
- [Terms](https://screenx.scrubmac.app/terms): Terms for downloading and using ScreenX.
- [Full LLM context](https://screenx.scrubmac.app/llms-full.txt): Extended product, privacy, release, and support details.

## Key Facts

- Product: ScreenX for macOS.
- Category: macOS utility / window manager / window snapping app.
- Platform: macOS 13 or later.
- Price: Free.
- License: MIT open source.
- Source repository: https://github.com/SyntaxFear/screenX
- Distribution: direct DMG download, Developer ID signed, Apple-notarized, and stapled.
- Current version: 1.0.2.
- Core workflow: hold Command while dragging a window to preview and place it.
- Quick layouts: halves, thirds, quarters, center, maximize, and two-thirds layouts.
- Display support: per-screen rows, columns, gaps, and saved display profiles.
- Workspace support: save and restore repeated window arrangements.
- Privacy: window arrangement happens locally on the user's Mac. ScreenX uses macOS Accessibility permission and does not need Screen Recording permission for its core workflow.
- Not affiliated with Apple.
